text:
Purpose: quick, practical plan for the company halfway checkpoint — align exec updates, finalize a trimmed OKR set for Q3/Q4 cadence, and provide a short run-of-show + action owners for the all-hands. This is a working playbook not the final deck. Keep it tight and actionable.

High-level goals for this doc:
- Surface 3 cross-company priorities to feature in the all-hands (no more than 3).
- Propose a condensed OKR set for public sharing (top-line only).
- Produce a run-of-show (15–20 minute core + breakouts).
- Assign owners and next steps for everything that requires follow-up before the townhall.

Context / constraints:
- Budget posture: small discretionary spend available for Q3 pilots but hiring freeze still on engineering ICs (exceptions reviewed by CFO).

title: Compact role proof assignment and day-one checklist
preview:
Purpose: quick, low-friction way to validate candidate fit for small IC roles (eg. Platform/Infra/Serving) where we want a 72-hour signal + a succinct day-one onboarding plan. Use when we need faster decisions than a multi-week take-home + full onsite loop.

High-level flow (target: 10 business days from screen to o...
text:
Purpose: quick, low-friction way to validate candidate fit for small IC roles (eg. Platform/Infra/Serving) where we want a 72-hour signal + a succinct day-one onboarding plan. Use when we need faster decisions than a multi-week take-home + full onsite loop.

High-level flow (target: 10 business days from screen to offer/no-go):
- Day 0: Recruiter screen, confirm availability for 72-hour trial and right-to-work/KYC checks.
- Day 1-3: Compact proof assignment (candidate given 72 hours to complete a constrained task).
- Day 4: Hiring manager + reviewer readout (30–45m).
- Day 5: Practical interview (60m) focused on design + operational judgement.
- Day 6–7: Reference checks + final calibration.
- Day 8–10: Offer / close loop.

Goals for this doc:
- Standardize the compact assignment instructions and grading sheet.
- Provide a simple day-one checklist for hires who come through this path ...

text:
Purpose:
This doc is a compact bridge between exec priorities and the all-staff narrative for the Q2→Q3 transition.
It is intentionally terse: use for rehearsal notes, talking trajectories for founders/ELT, and the canonical list of asks we cascade to teams this week.

Executive summary (1 paragraph):
We will focus the next quarter on three cross-cutting themes: (1) Make inference economics explicit — measurable unit cost improvements without degrading quality; (2) Harden reliability and predictable fallbacks across Hosted/Dedicated/Private; (3) Improve developer onboarding velocity for 3rd-party integrators. These map to the OKR draft below.

High-level timeline (milestones):
- Feb 2: Exec review of OKR seeds (this doc).
- Feb 9: Leadership sync — finalize objectives and critical signals.
- Feb 16: Leadership message drafted; slides + rehearsal plan ready.
- Feb 23: All-staff comms +...

title: Micro-certification Track and 72hr Try-On Activation
preview:
Purpose:
This doc lays out a lightweight micro-certification track (skill badges + short assessments) paired with a 72-hour candidate ‘try-on’ activation for high-velocity hiring pockets (platform infra, support SRE, and junior inference engineers). Goal: reduce time-to-productivity, improve signal before offers, an...
text:
Purpose:
This doc lays out a lightweight micro-certification track (skill badges + short assessments) paired with a 72-hour candidate ‘try-on’ activation for high-velocity hiring pockets (platform infra, support SRE, and junior inference engineers). Goal: reduce time-to-productivity, improve signal before offers, and create a standardized, fair rubric for convert/decline decisions.

Who this is for:
- Hiring managers and interviewers in eng/platform/SRE pods
- People Ops recruiters running fast cycles
- Mentors who will run the 72hr try-on

High level flow (timeline):
1) Recruiter screens -> short coding/knowledge pre-badge (24–48h)
2) Micro-cert assessment (remote, 45m) -> badge awarded if >=70%
3) Invite to 72hr paid try-on (candidate works with a mentor on a small scoped task)
4) Mentor debrief + rubric scoring -> hiring decision window (48h after try-on ends)

-...

title: First-Week Sandbox and Role-Proving Sprint
preview:

This doc describes a lightweight first-week sandbox designed to both (a) give candidates a fast, real-feel task to demonstrate role fit and (b) bootstrap a new hire's first-week momentum if hired. text:
Purpose:
This doc describes a lightweight first-week sandbox designed to both (a) give candidates a fast, real-feel task to demonstrate role fit and (b) bootstrap a new hire's first-week momentum if hired. The approach sits between a 72hr trial and a full takehome: short, timeboxed, mentored, and explicitly scoped for a hiring decision + immediate onboarding carryover.

Scope (who/use-cases):
- Roles: Individual contributor engineers (infra/platform/serving-runtime), SRE, small-ish product-engineering hires, and solutions engineers. Not intended for senior ICs > L6 or managers (use extended takehome + onsite).
- When to use: Final-stage candidates when we need faster validation of day-to-day signals (ownership, debugging, triage, communication) and when team bandwidth to run a small scaffolded trial exists.

High-level timeline:
- Day -2 (pre-week): Candidate receives sandbox brief, a...

title: Approval playtest: synthetic evidence protocol
preview:
Purpose
---
This is a lightweight PRD-style playbook for a focused playtest: exercise approval gates and audit-log retention semantics in Private deployments using synthetic (fabricated) change events. The goal is to validate operational assumptions, measure evidence size/provenance, and exercise customer-managed-ne...
text:
Purpose
---
This is a lightweight PRD-style playbook for a focused playtest: exercise approval gates and audit-log retention semantics in Private deployments using synthetic (fabricated) change events. The goal is to validate operational assumptions, measure evidence size/provenance, and exercise customer-managed-network handshakes without touching real customer configs.

Why this helps
---
- Low-risk way to verify that approval sampling, compact evidence bundles, and rollback tokens behave end-to-end in on-prem environments.
- Lets us measure network cost, archive size, and query latency for common forensic queries before committing to a retention/ingest model.
- Provides concrete UX/automation gaps for operator-side agents and control-plane attestation.

Scope (what we will test)
---
1) Approval gate flow: generate a sequence of synthetic VPC-change requests that exercise each appro...

Purpose:
This doc is a working brief for the November–January company cadence experiment we're calling the ‘Campus Huddle’. It bundles a short all-staff forum, an exec sneak-preview, and a cross-functional commitment matrix so teams leave with concrete 30/60/90 commitments.

TL;DR:
- Why: tighten alignment between product roadmaps, infra capacity, and go-to-market for the next quarter so we ship predictable throughput improvements and a prioritized customer onboarding pilot.
- What: 90-minute live campus-huddle (hybrid) + 5 follow-up micro-sessions for handoffs.
- Outcome: signed commitment matrix, 3 company-level OKRs in draft, and 2 immediate exec asks.

Audience:
- All staff invited; primary listeners: Eng (serving-runtime, infra, SRE), Product (hosted-api, dedicated), GTM (growth + enterprise), Customer Success.

Purpose:
This doc lays out a lightweight, mentor-led onboarding playbook specifically for new-graduate hires (new grads, recent interns converting to FTE, and entry-level ICs). Goal: accelerate time-to-impact, increase retention for early-career cohorts, create consistent evaluation signals for conversion decisions.

- People Ops + Hiring Managers + Mentors + new-grad hires
- Applies to Engineering and Product entry-level roles first phase; will adapt for SRE, DX, and Solutions in Q2 pilot

High-level objectives:
- 30-day ramp: core repo familiarity, local dev flow, first minor PR landed
- 60-day ramp: ownership of a small bug/feature, independent debugging workflow
- 90-day ramp: clear promotion/conversion signal or development plan; mentor/manager alignment on outcomes
- Mentor satisfaction and mentee NPS > 7 (post-90-day survey)

title: Account Synthesis — SLI Playbook for Customer Reviews
preview:
Context and intent
This is a lightweight, opinionated playbook we can use when prepping account-level review materials (monthly/quarterly). Goal: ensure CS, Solutions Eng, and Observability teams speak the same measurement language and can quickly derive flags for renewal/expansion conversations.

High-level rules o...
text:
Context and intent
This is a lightweight, opinionated playbook we can use when prepping account-level review materials (monthly/quarterly). Goal: ensure CS, Solutions Eng, and Observability teams speak the same measurement language and can quickly derive flags for renewal/expansion conversations.

High-level rules of engagement (notes to self)
- Use the same 30/90/365 windows consistently across accounts in a single review; prefer 30d as the short window, 90d for trend, 365d for annualized anomalies.
- Percentile latency semantics: explicit about request types (generate vs embed vs rerank) and sequence length buckets.
- For private/dedicated customers call out any on-prem telemetry gaps (we may need agent logs vs control-plane metrics).
- When in doubt, flag and attach raw time-series for engineering to investigate — don’t guess on remediation steps in a CS deck.

Metric definitions (...

Context and intent:
This is a compact, low-friction onboarding and interview-evidence bundle intended for small IC hires (engineer / infra / platform) who will be given a quiet/solo launch for their first 15 days. The goal is to reduce cognitive load, create a defensible skill signal for cross-functional partners, and accelerate contributor impact while keeping mentor time bounded. Draft for People Ops + hiring manager alignment before piloting with 2 hires.

High-level goals:
- First 15 days: remove >80% of non-essential access friction, ship 1 microtask to prod or customer-facing integration, and capture structured evidence for performance calibration.
- Mentor time budget: 3–4 hours/week active pairing + asynchronous feedback.
- Hiring alignment: ensure interview evidence maps to day-1–15 success signals so we can correlate interview rubric to ramp outcomes.

preview:

text:
Purpose: quick decision-support for short-term hiring sprints (2–6 weeks) that balances business impact, role difficulty, and candidate fill-time. Working doc: use this during weekly recruiting syncs and interview calibration sessions.

Notes to self / context:
- Many teams need faster TTM for high-leverage ICs (infra, serving runtime, SRE). We only have limited recruiter hours and interview slots.
- Objective: create a lightweight, repeatable way to prioritize reqs and a small bank of behavioral probes that maps to our competency rubric.
- Keep interview probes short (30–90s read) so interviewers can land follow-ups rapidly.

1) Recruit Prioritization matrix (one-pager)
- Inputs (for each open req): business impact (1–5), time-to-value (weeks estimate), role difficulty (1–5), market scarcity (1–5), internal ramp cost (1–5).
- Scoring: weighted sum = (impact * 3) + (1 / time-to-value ...

This is a working doc for an experimental cross-role \"talent rotation\" hiring and onboarding pattern intended for early-stage hires and lateral ICs joining cross-functional pods. High-level flow (timeline):
1) Screen -> take-home (optional) -> panel interviews (2 rounds) -> decision within 7 business days.

Purpose:
This is a compact, pragmatic 45-day onboarding playbook tailored for cross-functional hires (PMs, Solutions Engineers, Data Analysts, Product Designers) who need a fast ramp into product, customers, and engineering partners. The goal is to provide a minimal set of high-impact activities, recurring micro-rituals, and a lightweight evidence rubric so managers and new hires know what ‘on track’ looks like.

Scope and assumptions:
- Applies to mid-level individual contributors (IC3–IC4 equivalent).
- Emphasizes asynchronous learning + short live syncs to reduce meeting load.
- Designed for hires joining product-adjacent teams who will interact with eng, infra, and customer success.

Purpose:
This playbook is a practical, low-friction plan for converting high-performing contractors into permanent employees with a focus on early integration, calibrated interview scoring, and manager-led retention touchpoints. It assumes conversion is driven by performance and business need rather than a simple time-based trigger.

Scope and audience:
- Audience: people-ops, hiring managers, recruiting partners, hiring panel members, and the conversions review committee.
- Scope: contractors in IC and engineering-adjacent roles (SWE, SRE, Applied ML infra, Product/PM), for mid-to-senior levels. Not intended for executive-level conversions (separate approval flow).

Why a different approach?
Contractor conversions are high-leverage hires: these candidates already understand product and culture. This playbook reduces duplication (don’t repeat full external hire loop), focuses intervie...

text:
Purpose:
This playbook is designed to standardize the first 30 days for new ICs and managers at Redwood Inference. It focuses on fast functional ramp, clear handoffs, and measurable micro-goals so hiring managers and buddies can coordinate without many back-and-forths.

Scope:
Applies to all full-time engineering hires, applied ML hires, and product managers hired into pods. For contractors and interns, use the abbreviated checklist (lower priority for system access and long-term comms).

Quick overview (one-line):
- Day 0: access, welcome, intro to pod and buddy assignment.
- Day 1–7: orientation sprints, 2 small measurable tasks, key readings and infra walkthrough.
- Day 8–30: stakeholder meetings, first PR, first oncall shadow if relevant, first 1:1s established.

Hiring rubric (to attach to offer & interview packet):
We use a weighted rubric to make offers consistent across hiring...

text:
Purpose:
This doc outlines a lightweight, skills-first credentialing flow for mid-to-senior IC hires that shortens time-to-decision while improving onboarding signal. The goal is a reproducible pilot that: reduces interview overload, creates verifiable micro-credentials, and maps hiring outcomes directly to onboarding unlocks.

Scope and constraints:
- Pilot scope: backend, infra, and ML-serving adjacent ICs (eng levels IC3–IC5). Not aimed at non-engineering roles in this first phase.
- Timebox: 3-month pilot starting Sept 2025 with 30 hires cap.
- Privacy: candidate artifacts stored in the private hiring gdrive folder and SSO-protected Confluence page.
- Non-binary signal: we use granular pass thresholds per credential rather than a single pass/fail score.

High-level flow (candidate-facing):
1) Quick intake (30m) — recruiter screen to verify role fit and constraints.
2) Micro take-h...

title: Contributor Growth Pathway & Interview Lite Rubric
preview:
Purpose:
This doc combines a short, practical contributor growth pathway (onboarding + 30/90 plan) with an \"interview-lite\" rubric for fast-moving hiring loops. Goal: reduce ambiguity in early-stage offers, speed up decisions for small teams, and provide managers a repeatable first-90-days template.

Scope / When ...
text:
Purpose:
This doc combines a short, practical contributor growth pathway (onboarding + 30/90 plan) with an \"interview-lite\" rubric for fast-moving hiring loops. Goal: reduce ambiguity in early-stage offers, speed up decisions for small teams, and provide managers a repeatable first-90-days template.

Scope / When to use:
- Use for IC hires (E3-E5 equivalent) and internal lateral moves where we want a lightweight loop (2-3 interviews) instead of a full multi-panel process.
- Not for senior ICs (staff/principal) or leadership hires — those keep the full rubric.

Why this exists (rationale):
- Hiring velocity: small teams need quick decisions.
- Predictability: ensure consistent expectations for ramp and early performance.
- Signal alignment: capture enough data to evaluate craft + team fit without over-indexing on whiteboard perfection.

Purpose and scope:
This is a lightweight, reusable kit for running a 72-hour practical assessment across roles (SWE/backend, ML engineer/infra, product manager, SRE/ops). The goal is to: 1) get a sense for hands-on problem solving and communication, 2) reduce onsite time by pushing practicals earlier, 3) provide consistent scoring and a minimal day-zero checklist so accepted candidates can be productive on day 1.

Intended use and constraints:
- Use for mid-to-senior IC hires (IC3-IC5).
- Volunteer-based practicals: candidate chooses to accept practical phase after initial screen.
- Should take ~6–10 hours total for candidate over 72 hours (split tasks + writeups).
- Not a take-home whiteboard replacement — emphasize realistic artifacts (small PR, infra terraform snippet, decision memo, runbook).
- Timeboxed and scaffolded to respect candidate bandwidth; compensation offered when >6 h...

Purpose:
This doc is a lightweight, operational playbook for a compact company-wide ‘Tempo Reset’ and leadership handoff to kick off Q3 planning. Aim is to provide a short, repeatable runbook that centers three things: clarity of North Star priorities, a compact OKR framing for each org, and a pragmatic set of asks for managers to execute in the following two weeks.

Context / Why now:
- After the product ramp and hiring bursts in H1, we’re seeing drift in cross-team focus and inconsistent rollout expectations.
- Leadership wants a fast, low-friction sync (not a long-form all-hands) to align on what to decouple, what to double-down on, and how to measure success for the next 6–10 weeks.
- This is intentionally tactical: align, handoff, and equip managers with specific deliverables.

Purpose
This doc outlines a compact, hands-on 1-week role immersion ‘hackathon’ used as both an advanced-stage interview and a new-hire rapid-onboard experiment. Goal: compress signal for senior ICs and senior engineers while giving candidates a real, scoped piece of work that mirrors the first 30 days on the team. It doubles as a starter sprint for hires who accept.

Why we run this
- Traditional take-home assignments can be noisy and disconnected. A short immersive week gives better signal about collaboration, prioritization, and code hygiene.
- Faster candidate experience (7 days vs multi-week loops).
- Doubles as realistic onboarding: accepted hires flip this week into their first deliverables.

Scope and target roles
- Best for senior software engineers, infra engineers, SRE candidates, and senior product/platform engineers. Not suited for junior grads or strictly PM-only roles.

Purpose:
This playbook provides a compact, asynchronous-first onboarding and handoff template for hires that will operate in a hybrid model (remote + periodic in-office). It is optimized for fast ramp and transfer of domain knowledge for contract-to-perm and cross-functional lateral moves. The document is intentionally checklist-driven so hiring managers, mentors, and new hires can run a 10-day rapid activation followed by a structured 30–90 day growth ladder with clear success signals.

Success criteria (example):
- New hire can independently complete the basic support flow / ship a small scoped change / run the weekly oncall runbook by day 30.
- Positive qualitative mentor feedback by week 6.
- Role-readiness score >= 80% on the role-competency checklist by day 90.

title: Activation Gap Intervention Runbook
preview:
Purpose:
This runbook defines a lightweight, operationally-focused health scoring approach for identifying customers that have an "activation gap"—i.e., accounts that have completed initial onboarding but show falling or inconsistent product activity that puts renewal at risk. The goal is to produce an actionable pe...
text:
Purpose:
This runbook defines a lightweight, operationally-focused health scoring approach for identifying customers that have an "activation gap"—i.e., accounts that have completed initial onboarding but show falling or inconsistent product activity that puts renewal at risk. The goal is to produce an actionable per-customer "Activation Score" and a short, prioritized intervention plan for CSMs and SEs.

Background and assumptions:
- We treat "activation" as a sustained, repeatable pattern of product usage following onboarding (not just a single success event).
- This worksheet is intended to be computed weekly and surfaced in the CSM scoreboard used for QBR planning and renewal forecasting.
- Scores map to operational tiers (Green/Watch/Intervene) with clear, time-boxed actions.
- The model is deliberately simple and interpretable so CSMs can explain the reason for outreach to custo...
